comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: FROM: Rohan Sharma, Aspen Institute Civic AI Leader Attached is a technical comment regarding the Air Quality State Implementation Plans; Approvals and Promulgations: Interstate Transport Plan Review for the 2015 Ozone NAAQS (Docket EPA-HQ-OAR-2025-0192). This response identifies critical APA vulnerabilities in framing the 1 ppb contribution threshold solely...

EPA-HQ-OAR-2025-0192 Interstate Transport Plan Review (2015 Ozone NAAQS) I support this proposed rule because I believe it properly enforces the Clean Air Acts good neighbor provision through review of state SIPs. To my understanding this rule is not changing the 70 ppb ozone limit but rather, it determines whether states have met their legal threshold to en...

I recommend this plan be denied. It is criminal to allow an increase of pollution when we already have 90,000 Americans die annually from pollution. The EPA is being mismanaged by the trump / republican administration, to the detriment of the environment and Americans health.

Chesapeake Bay Foundation, Clean Air Task Force, Earthjustice, Environmental Defense Fund, the Natural Resources Defense Council, the Sierra Club, and Southern Environmental Law Center respectfully request an extension of the public comment period for the Environmental Protection Agency’s rulemaking titled “Interstate Transport Plan Review for the 2015 Ozone...

comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: Chesapeake Climate Action Network Action Fund is in strong opposition to the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) rolling back the “Good Neighbor” pollution rule. Under this proposal, EPA would step back from overseeing cross-state air pollution — putting millions of people’s health at risk and shifting the burden onto downwind states.
